title: YouTube to roll out AI tools to ‘experiment with music’ this year - Music Ally
text:
YouTube CEO Neal Mohan has
published his ‘priorities for 2026’ message
, and it includes a reference to his company’s plans for AI-generated music. Albeit a very brief one.
“This year you’ll be able to create a Short using your own likeness, produce games with a simple text prompt, and experiment with music,” wrote Mohan. Thus far, YouTube’s AI-music tools have been restricted to a small group of musicians and creators.
Mohan also addressed concerns about ‘AI slop’ on YouTube, while pointing out that the platform is being relatively cautious with its moderation policies.
“Over the past 20 years, we’ve learned not to impose any preconceived notions on the creator ecosystem. Today, once-odd trends like ASMR and watching other people play video games are mainstream hits,” he wrote.
“But with this openness comes a responsibility to maintain the high quality viewing experience that people want.” This came with a promise to continue working to “reduce the spread of low quality AI content”.
Mohan also trailed plans to “bring even more variety to Shorts by integrating different formats – like image posts – directly into the feed” later this year, as well as music features “making it easier to discover and experience new releases”.
Stuart Dredge
Music Ally's Head of Insight
